On a failure, a creature takes 28 (8d6) poison damage and is poisoned for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t poisoned. While poisoned, a creature takes 3 (1d6) poison damage at the start of each of its turns. A poisoned creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Emptying a vial of holy water on the poisoned creature also ends the effect on it.

bonus.name: Startling Screech
entries: The vrock emits a horrific screech at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or be able to take either an action or bonus action on its turn, not both, and it can’t take reactions.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The wrack demon is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The wrack demon has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wrack demon makes three Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or have disadvantage on attack rolls as reality-warping energies of the Void temporarily distort its body.

action.name: Warping Burst (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The wrack demon unleashes a blast of Void energy that warps and distorts the area around the demon. Each creature within 20 feet of the demon must make a DC 15 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 27 (6d8) necrotic damage, its speed is reduced by 10 feet, and its AC is reduced by 2 until the end of its next turn, as its flesh twists and warps. On a success, a creature takes half the damage, and its speed and AC aren’t reduced.

bonus.name: Body Warping
entries: The wrack demon shifts its malleable form, rearranging limbs, flesh, and bone as needed to fulfill its tasks. It chooses one of the following effects, which lasts until the wrack demon uses this bonus action again:
• Bony Hide. The demon forces the shards of bone to the
surface of its skin, increasing its AC by 2. In addition, it has advantage on checks and saves against any effect that would move it.
• Infiltration. The demon changes the texture and
stickiness of its skin, gaining a climbing speed of 30 feet. In addition, it can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
• Scout. The demon twists its body into a quadrupedal
form, increasing its speed by 10 feet. In addition, opportunity attacks against it have disadvantage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The derro has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the derro has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 3 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Light Crossbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Otherworldly Feast
entries: When a creature the derro can see within 5 feet of it takes damage (including itself), the derro can magically absorb some of the life energy spilling from the creature. The creature that took the damage must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or the derro gains temporary HP equal to half the damage the creature took.

bonus.name: Intense Study
entries: The derro intensely studies one creature it can see within 30 feet of it, unnerving the creature. The target must succeed on a DC 12 WIS save or the derro has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against the target before the start of the derro’s next turn.

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: The void eater has advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its HP .

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The void eater has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Relentless (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the void eater takes 10 damage or less that would reduce it to 0 HP , it is reduced to 1 HP instead.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the void eater has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 3 when perceiving by sight.

trait.name: Void-Touched Weapons
entries: The void eater’s weapon attacks are magical. When the void eater hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 2d6 necrotic damage (included in the attack).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Claw attacks and one Tail attack, or it makes three Hurl Flame attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) fire damage. If the target is a flammable object that isn’t being worn or carried, it also catches fire.

bonus.name: Maddening Gaze
entries: The void eater intensely studies one creature it can see within 30 feet of it, purple Void energy flickering across its pale eyes. The target must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or be frightened of the void eater until the end of its next turn. A creature that fails this save by 5 or more suffers a random, short-term dread effect for 1 minute.

trait.name: Barbed Death
entries: When the barbed devil dies, its body withers, leaving behind a mass of spikes on the ground in a 10-foot radius centered on the devil’s body. The area becomes difficult terrain for 1 minute, and when a creature moves into or within the area, it takes 2d6 piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels.

trait.name: Barbed Hide
entries: A creature that hits the barbed devil with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 3 (1d6) piercing damage. A creature that starts its turn grappling or grappled by the barbed devil takes 3 (1d6) piercing damage.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Show Mettle
entries: While it can see a superior devil, such as a barbed devil, pit fiend, or similar, the bearded devil has advantage on saves against being charmed and frightened.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Beard Spines attacks and one Glaive attack.

action.name: Beard Spines
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. While poisoned in this way, the target can’t regain HP . The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Glaive
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) slashing damage. If the target is a creature other than a Construct or Undead, it must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or lose 3 (1d6) HP at the start of each of its turns due to an infernal wound. Each time the devil hits the wounded target with this attack, the damage dealt by the wound increases by 3 (1d6). Any creature can take an action to stanch the wound with a successful DC 10 WIS (Medicine) check. The wound also closes if the target receives magical healing, provided the target is capable of regaining HP .

bonus.name: Enhance Pain
entries: The bearded devil enhances the pain in one creature it can see within 30 feet of it that is poisoned from the devil’s Beard Spines attack or suffering an infernal wound from the devil’s Glaive attack. The target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn, as it doubles over in pain.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Claw attacks and one Sting attack.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Sting
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Devilish Command
entries: The bone devil shouts one of the following commands at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or obey the command. A lesser devil, such as a barbed devil or lemure, has disadvantage on this save.
• Attack. The target uses its reaction to make one melee
or ranged attack (the devil’s choice) against one creature the target can see within its weapon’s range.
• Beg for Mercy. The target immediately falls prone and
can’t cast spells with verbal components on its next turn as it is magically compelled to speak pleas of mercy.
• Flee. The target uses its reaction to move up to its speed
directly away from the bone devil. This movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks, but the target doesn’t avoid damaging terrain, such as lava.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes four Chain attacks.

action.name: Chain
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) piercing damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 15). Until the grapple ends, the target is restrained and takes 14 (4d6) piercing damage at the start of each of its turns, and the chain devil can’t use the same Chain on another target.

bonus.name: Animate Chains
entries: The chain devil commands chains it can see within 60 feet of it that aren’t being worn or carried to grab up to two creatures it can see within 10 feet of at least one commanded chain. Each creature must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be restrained. A creature, including the restrained creature, can take its action to free the restrained creature by succeeding on a DC 15 STR check. The devil can have up to two creatures restrained in this way at a time. If it uses this bonus action to restrain a third, the effect ends on one of the previous targets (the devil’s choice).

reaction.name: Unnerving Mask
entries: When a creature the devil can see starts its turn within 30 feet of the devil, the devil can command its mask to shift to look like one of the creature’s departed loved ones or bitter enemies. If the creature can see the mask, it must succeed on a DC 15 WIS save or be frightened until the end of its turn.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Disciplined Fighter
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the erinyes chooses one of the following fighting styles to adopt, which lasts until the start of the erinyes’s next turn.
• Aggressive. The erinyes has advantage on the first attack
it makes on its turn, but the next attack roll against the erinyes has advantage.
• Retributive. The next time the erinyes is hit by a melee
weapon attack from a creature within 5 feet of it, the erinyes can make one Longsword attack against the attacker (no action required).
• Defensive. The next time erinyes uses the Parry reaction,
it takes only half the damage if the attack hits and regains 18 (4d8) HP if the attack doesn’t hit.

trait.name: Poisonous Weapons
entries: The erinyes’s weapon attacks are magical. When the erinyes hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 4d8 poison damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The erinyes has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The erinyes makes three Longsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) slashing damage, or 9 (1d10 + 4) slashing damage if used with two hands, plus 18 (4d8) poison damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage plus 18 (4d8) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The erinyes adds 4 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the erinyes must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Fork attacks and one Tail attack, or it makes three Hurl Flame attacks.

action.name: Fork
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (3d8 + 6) piercing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) piercing damage. If the target is a creature other than a Construct or Undead, it must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or lose 10 (3d6) HP at the start of each of its turns due to an infernal wound. Each time the devil hits the wounded target with this attack, the damage dealt by the wound increases by 10 (3d6). Any creature can take an action to stanch the wound with a successful DC 12 WIS (Medicine) check. The wound also closes if the target receives magical healing.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (5d6 + 3) fire damage. If the target is a flammable object that isn’t being worn or carried, it also catches fire.

bonus.name: Headbutt
entries: The horned devil slams its horned head into a creature it can see within 5 feet of it. The target must make a DC 17 STR save. On a failure, the target takes 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a success, the target takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Snow Walk
entries: The devil can move across icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check. In addition, difficult terrain composed of ice or snow doesn’t cost it extra movement.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Claw attacks and one Tail attack, or it makes three Icicle Shard attacks.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) slashing damage plus 18 (4d8) cold damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (3d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 18 (4d8) cold damage.

action.name: Icicle Shard
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage plus 18 (4d8) cold damage.

bonus.name: Ice Wall
entries: The ice devil causes a wall of ice to magically rise from the ground at a point it can see within 60 feet of it. The wall is up to 30 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick. It can be any shape, as long as its base is connected to the ground. If the wall cuts through a creature’s space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side (the devil’s choice). If a creature would be surrounded on all sides by the wall, it can make a DC 17 DEX save. On a success, it can use its reaction to move up to its speed so that it is no longer enclosed by the wall. The wall lasts 1 minute, until the devil creates a new wall, or until the devil dies. Each 10-foot section of the wall has AC 5, 30 HP , is vulnerable to fire damage, and is immune to acid, cold, necrotic, poison, and psychic damage.

reaction.name: Ice Shield
entries: When the ice devil or a friendly creature it can see is hit by an attack, the devil can create a magical barrier of ice to disrupt the attack. The target halves the damage from the attack.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the imp’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The imp has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Slam (Beast Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (based on the type of damage dealt by the Beast form’s primary attack, such as Bite).

action.name: Sting (True Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage, and the target must make a DC 12 CON save, taking 10 (3d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Invisibility (True Form Only)
entries: The imp magically turns invisible until it attacks or uses Change Shape, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the imp wears or carries is invisible with it.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The imp magically transforms into a Medium or smaller Beast that has a CR no higher than its own or back into its true form, which is a Fiend. Without wings, it loses its flying speed. Its statistics, other than its size and speed, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the lemure’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Hellish Rejuvenation
entries: Unlike other outsiders, a lemure that dies in its home plane of the Hells is restored in a new body in 1d10 days, regaining all its HP and becoming active again. The new body appears in the Hells within 1 mile of where it was slain. Only sprinkling holy water on the lemure’s remains can prevent this trait from functioning.

action.name: Fist
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage plus 2 (1d4) fire damage.

bonus.name: Rippling Heat (1/Day)
entries: The lemure exudes an aura of extreme heat for 1 minute. Each creature that starts its turn within 15 feet of the lemure and that isn’t a devil must succeed on a DC 11 CON save or be weakened until the start of its next turn. A weakened creature has disadvantage on all attack rolls, and it deals only half damage with weapon attacks that use Strength. A creature that fails this save by 5 or more suffers one level of exhaustion. A creature that has suffered a level of exhaustion from this lemure’s Rippling Heat can’t suffer another level from this lemure’s Rippling Heat for 24 hours.

trait.name: Aura of the Devil Lord
entries: Each hostile creature that starts its turn within 20 feet of the pit fiend must succeed on a DC 21 WIS save or be frightened until the start of its next turn. Each friendly devil within 20 feet of the pit fiend is immune to the charmed and frightened conditions and has advantage on the first attack roll it makes on each of its turns.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Flaming Weapons
entries: The pit fiend’s weapon attacks are magical. When the fiend hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 4d6 fire damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The pit fiend has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The pit fiend makes one Bite attack, one Gore attack, one Mace attack, and one Tail attack, or it makes four Hurl Flame attacks. If two Hurl Flame attacks hit one creature, each creature within 10 feet of the target must make a DC 21 DEX save as the fire bursts outward from the target, taking 14 (4d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d6 + 8) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) fire damage and 13 (3d8) poison damage. The target must succeed on a DC 21 CON save or be poisoned. While poisoned in this way, the target can’t regain HP . The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Gore
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d8 + 8) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) fire damage.

action.name: Mace
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d6 + 8) bludgeoning damage plus 14 (4d6) fire damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (3d10 + 8) bludgeoning damage plus 14 (4d6) fire damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 21 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +10 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 25 (6d6 + 4) fire damage. Dinosaurs T owering trees shake, losing leaves, and the ground rumbles as massive lizards walk across the land. A roar shatters the air, and a large-headed carnivore with teeth the length of longswords bursts out of the trees and bites at a grazing three-horned lizard.

trait.name: Hardy
entries: Any critical hit against the dinosaur becomes a normal hit.

trait.name: Hardy
entries: Any critical hit against the dinosaur becomes a normal hit.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The plesiosaurus can hold its breath for 1 hour.

trait.name: Hardy
entries: Any critical hit against the dinosaur becomes a normal hit.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ankylosaurus makes two Tail attacks.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 14 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Tail Sweep (Recharge 4–6)
entries: The ankylosaurus sweeps its tail, knocking over nearby creatures in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 14 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 27 (6d8) bludgeoning damage and is pushed up to 15 feet away and knocked prone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t pushed or knocked prone.

reaction.name: Defensive Huddle
entries: When a creature the ankylosaurus can see hits it with a weapon attack, the ankylosaurus hunkers down, shifting its body so that its armored back intercepts the blow. The ankylosaurus halves the damage from the attack.

trait.name: Hardy
entries: Any critical hit against the dinosaur becomes a normal hit.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The triceratops makes two Gore attacks.

action.name: Gore
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (3d8 + 6) piercing damage.

action.name: Stomp
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one prone creature. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Warning Bugle
entries: The triceratops emits a piercing call in a 30-foot cone, signaling its impending attack. Each creature in the area must succeed on a DC 14 WIS save or be frightened until the end of its next turn. If the triceratops uses this bonus action before moving the requisite 20 feet for Trampling Charge, the target of its charge has disadvantage on this save, and the triceratops can make a Stomp attack against the prone target without needing to use a bonus action, provided the target fails the STR save from the charge. 20 feet in a random direction and knocked prone. If the thrown target strikes a solid surface, the target takes 3 (1d6) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a DC 16 DEX save or take the same damage and be knocked prone.

trait.name: Hardy
entries: Any critical hit against the dinosaur becomes a normal hit.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The velociraptor has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of its allies is within 5 feet of the target and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The velociraptor makes one Bite attack and one Claws attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 12). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and the velociraptor can’t Bite another target.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) slashing damage.

action.name: Maul
entries: The velociraptor uses its claws to rip into one creature it is grappling. The target must make a DC 12 STR save, taking 10 (4d4) slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Convincing Mimicry (Humanoid Form Only)
entries: The doppelganger can mimic any Humanoid’s body and voice it has seen and heard nearly perfectly. A creature very familiar with the individual Humanoid the doppelganger is pretending to be can tell the doppelganger is an imitation with a successful DC 20 WIS (Insight) check.

trait.name: Revealed Intentions
entries: While reading a creature’s mind, the doppelganger has advantage on CHA checks against the creature, knows the creature’s current mood, and knows if the creature speaks a lie. In addition, the doppelganger has advantage on attack rolls against the creature.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The doppelganger makes two Slam or Psychic Bolt attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Psychic Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +4 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d10 + 2) psychic damage.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The doppelganger transforms into a Small or Medium Humanoid it has seen or back into its true form, which is Monstrosity. Its statistics, other than its size and speed, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Read Thoughts
entries: The doppelganger magically reads the surface thoughts—what is foremost in the mind at the moment—of one creature within 60 feet of it. The effect can penetrate barriers, but 3 feet of wood or dirt, 2 feet of stone, 2 inches of metal, or a thin sheet of lead blocks it. While the target is in range, the doppelganger can continue reading the target’s thoughts until its concentration is broken (as if concentrating on a spell) or it uses Read Thoughts on a different target.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The dragon turtle can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The dragon turtle deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Acid Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales acid in a 15-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 11 DEX save, taking 22 (5d8) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

reaction.name: Siege Follow-Through
entries: If the dragon turtle destroys an object or structure with an attack, it can make a Bite or Tail attack against a creature it can see within 5 feet of that object or structure.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The black dragon can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the dragon moves at least 15 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the dragon can make one Claw attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The dragon can breathe air and water.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Acid Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales acid in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 15 DEX save, taking 49 (11d8) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Acidic Vapors
entries: After the black dragon uses its Acid Breath, acid clings to its mouth, throat, and nostrils for a time, evaporating as the dragon breathes. While the dragon’s Acid Breath is unavailable, acidic vapors surround it, and each creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be poisoned until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Acidic Vapors
entries: After the black dragon uses its Acid Breath, acid clings to its mouth, throat, and nostrils for a time, evaporating as the dragon breathes. While the dragon’s Acid Breath is unavailable, acidic vapors surround it, and each creature that starts its turn within 20 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 18 CON save or be poisoned until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The dragon can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the dragon fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) acid damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d6 + 7) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d8 + 7) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the dragon’s choice that is within 120 feet of the dragon and aware of it must succeed on a DC 16 WIS save or be frightened for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature’s save is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon’s Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Acid Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales acid in a 60-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 18 DEX save, taking 49 (11d8) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The dragon can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The dragon regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The dragon automatically detects every creature and trap within 30 feet of it, pinpointing the current location of each.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The dragon makes a Tail attack.

legendary.name: Elemental Roar (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon roars at up to two creatures of its choice within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 16 CHA save or be vulnerable to acid damage until the end of its next turn. A target resistant to acid damage isn’t resistant to it for the duration. A target immune to acid damage is still immune to it, even if the target fails the save.

legendary.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 10 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 20 DEX save or take 14 (2d6 + 7) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

trait.name: Acidic Vapors
entries: After the black dragon uses its Acid Breath, acid clings to its mouth, throat, and nostrils for a time, evaporating as the dragon breathes. While the dragon’s Acid Breath is unavailable, acidic vapors surround it, and each creature that starts its turn within 30 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 21 CON save or be poisoned until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The dragon can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the dragon fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (2d10 + 9) piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) acid damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d6 + 9) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d8 + 9) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the dragon’s choice that is within 120 feet of the dragon and aware of it must succeed on a DC 18 WIS save or be frightened for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature’s save is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon’s Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Acid Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales acid in a 90-foot line that is 10 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 21 DEX save, taking 67 (15d8) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The dragon can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The dragon regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The dragon automatically detects every creature and trap within 30 feet of it, pinpointing the current location of each.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The dragon makes a Tail attack.

legendary.name: Elemental Roar (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon roars at up to two creatures of its choice within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 18 CHA save or be vulnerable to acid damage until the end of its next turn. A target resistant to acid damage isn’t resistant to it for the duration. A target immune to acid damage is still immune to it, even if the target fails the save.

legendary.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 15 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 23 DEX save or take 16 (2d6 + 9) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the dragon moves at least 15 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the dragon can make one Claw attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) lightning damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Lightning Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales lightning in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 12 DEX save, taking 27 (5d10) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Charged Hide
entries: When the blue dragon hasn’t exhaled its Lightning Breath, the electric charge builds up across its body. trait.name: Suffocating Heat
entries: After the brass dragon uses its Fire Breath, the air around it is devoid of moisture and lacking in breathable air. A creature that fails this save for two consecutive rounds falls unconscious until it takes damage or another creature uses an action to wake it.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The dragon magically transforms into a Beast or Humanoid that has a challenge rating no higher than its own, or back into its true form, which is Dragon. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it or is borne by the new form (the dragon’s choice). It reverts to its true form if it dies. In a new form, the dragon retains its HP , ability to speak, proficiencies, and INT, WIS, and CHA scores, as well as this bonus action. Its statistics and capabilities are otherwise replaced by those of the new form, except any class features or legendary actions of that form.

action.name: Slowing Breath (Recharge 6)
entries: The dragon exhales slowing gas in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 11 CON save. On a failure, the creature can’t use reactions, its speed is halved, and it can’t make more than one attack on its turn. In addition, the creature can use either an action or a bonus action on its turn, not both. These effects last for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Corrosive Scales
entries: When the copper dragon hasn’t spit its Acid Breath, the acid built up within the dragon causes the patina on its scales to spread to weapons that strike it. While the dragon’s Acid Breath is available, any weapon made of metal that hits the dragon corrodes. After dealing damage, the weapon takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to damage rolls. If a nonmagical weapon’s penalty drops to −5, the weapon is destroyed. Nonmagical ammunition made of metal that hits the dragon is destroyed after dealing damage. A magical weapon can’t have a penalty greater than −5, and a magical weapon removes this penalty after 24 hours or after its attuned wielder finishes a long rest.

entries: The dragon makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks. It can replace its Bite attack with a use of Slowing Breath, if available.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The dragon magically transforms into a Beast or Humanoid that has a challenge rating no higher than its own, or back into its true form, which is Dragon. entries: The dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 10 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 22 DEX save or take 15 (2d6 + 8) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

trait.name: Boil Over
entries: When the red dragon hasn’t unleashed its Fire Breath, the heat builds and rolls outward from it. While the dragon’s Fire Breath is available, it emits immense heat, and each creature that starts its turn within 30 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 24 CON save or take 7 (2d6) fire damage. A creature that fails this save by 5 or more also suffers one level of exhaustion.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the dragon fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (2d10 + 10) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) fire damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d6 + 10) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (2d8 + 10) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the dragon’s choice that is within 120 feet of the dragon and aware of it must succeed on a DC 21 WIS save or be frightened for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature’s save is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon’s Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Fire Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales fire in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 24 DEX save, taking 105 (30d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The dragon can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The dragon regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Detect

entries: The dragon roars at up to two creatures of its choice within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 21 CHA save or be vulnerable to fire damage until the end of its next turn. entries: If the dragon moves at least 15 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 14 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the dragon can make one Claw attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Paralyzing Breath (Recharge 6)
entries: The dragon exhales paralyzing gas in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be paralyzed for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Freezing Fog
entries: After the silver dragon uses its Cold Breath, freezing fog surrounds it. While the dragon’s Cold Breath is unavailable, each creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or be restrained until the start of its next turn.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The dragon magically transforms into a 14 4 DD Beast or Humanoid that has a challenge rating no higher than its own, or back into its true form, which is Dragon. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it or is borne by the new form (the dragon’s choice). It reverts to its true form if it dies. In a new form, the dragon retains its HP , ability to speak, proficiencies, and INT, WIS, and CHA scores, as well as this bonus action. Its statistics and capabilities are otherwise replaced by those of the new form, except any class features or legendary actions of that form.

trait.name: Void Dweller
entries: The void dragon doesn’t require air, food, drink, sleep, or ambient pressure, and it is immune to the effects of Void terrain. While traveling in space, the dragon magically glides on solar winds, covering immense distances in short times.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

action.name: Breath Weapons (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The void dragon uses one of the following breath weapons.
• Gravitic Breath. The dragon exhales localized gravity

in a 15-foot cube, originating from the dragon. When a creature ends a fall in the cube, it takes 5 (1d10) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it fell to a maximum of 20d10 bludgeoning damage, instead of normal falling damage. A creature that enters the cube for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must make a DC 13 DEX save. On a failure, the creature is restrained. On a success, the creature’s speed is halved while it remains in the cube. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The cube lasts until the start of the dragon’s next turn.
• Withering Breath. The dragon exhales the life-rending
energy of the Void in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 13 DEX save, taking 21 (6d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d8 + 8) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the dragon’s choice that is within 120 feet of the dragon and aware of it must succeed on a DC 17 WIS save or be frightened for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature’s save is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon’s Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Cold Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales an icy blast in a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 22 CON save, taking 63 (14d8) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The dragon can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The dragon regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The dragon automatically detects every creature and trap within 30 feet of it, pinpointing the current location of each.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The dragon makes a Tail attack.

legendary.name: Elemental Roar (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon roars at up to two creatures of its choice within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 17 CHA save or be vulnerable to cold damage until the end of its next turn. A target resistant to cold damage isn’t resistant to it for the duration. A target immune to cold damage is still immune to it, even if the target fails the save.

legendary.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 15 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 22 DEX save or take 15 (2d6 + 8) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

trait.name: Grasslands Camouflage
entries: The yellow dragon’s Stealth is 19 while in tall grass or foliage.

trait.name: Pounce
entries: If the dragon moves at least 15 feet straight toward a creature and then hits with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 12 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the dragon can make one Claw attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) radiant damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Radiant Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales radiant energy in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a DC 12 DEX save, taking 27 (6d8) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Blinding Sunlight
entries: When the dragon hasn’t released its Radiant Breath, streaks of sunlight pour from its mouth. Bright light within 10 feet of the dragon is sunlight, and each creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be blinded until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Grasslands Camouflage
entries: The yellow dragon’s Stealth is 20 while in tall grass or foliage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) radiant damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) slashing damage.

action.name: Radiant Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales radiant energy in a 60-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a DC 15 DEX save, taking 45 (10d8) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Blinding Sunlight
entries: When the dragon hasn’t released its Radiant Breath, streaks of sunlight pour from its mouth. Bright light within 20 feet of the dragon is sunlight, and each creature that starts its turn within 20 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 19 CON save or be blinded until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Grasslands Camouflage
entries: The yellow dragon’s Stealth is 22 while in tall grass or foliage.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the yellow dragon fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) radiant damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d6 + 7) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d8 + 7) bludgeoning damage.

A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature’s save is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the dragon’s Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

entries: The dragon exhales radiant energy in a 90-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a DC 19 DEX save, taking 54 (12d8) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries: The dragon uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (2d10 + 9) piercing damage plus 13 (3d8) radiant damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d6 + 9) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d8 + 9) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Radiant Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The dragon exhales radiant energy in a 120-foot line that is 10 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a DC 22 DEX save, taking 81 (18d8) radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The dragon can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The dragon regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The dragon automatically detects every creature and trap within 30 feet of it, pinpointing the current location of each.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The dragon makes a Tail attack.

legendary.name: Elemental Roar (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon roars at up to two creatures of its choice within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 20 CHA save or be vulnerable to radiant damage until the end of its next turn. A target resistant to radiant damage isn’t resistant to it for the duration. A target immune to radiant damage is still immune to it, even if the target fails the save.

legendary.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The dragon beats its wings. Each creature within 10 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 23 DEX save or take 16 (2d6 + 9) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. The dragon can then fly up to half its flying speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

monster.name: Pseudodragon
languages: understands Common and Draconic but can’t speak

trait.name: Heightened Senses
entries: The pseudodragon’s Perception is 18 when perceiving by sight, hearing, or smell.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The pseudodragon has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Limited Telepathy
entries: The pseudodragon can magically communicate simple ideas, emotions, and images telepathically with any creature within 100 feet of it that can understand a language.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Sting
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) piercing damage plus 2 (1d4) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 11 CON save or be poisoned for 1 hour. If the creature fails the save by 5 or more, it falls unconscious while poisoned in this way, or until it takes damage or another creature uses an action to shake it awake.

action.name: Puff of Smoke (1/Day)
entries: The pseudodragon exhales a cloud of smoke in a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on itself. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. It lasts for 1 minute or until a wind of moderate or greater speed (at least 10 miles per hour) disperses it.

trait.name: Heat Shimmer
entries: Creatures more than 30 feet from the scorch drake have disadvantage on WIS (Perception) checks to see the drake.

trait.name: Scalding Blood
entries: A creature that hits the scorch drake with a melee attack while within 5 feet of the drake takes 3 (1d6) fire damage and must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or ignite. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, an ignited creature takes 3 (1d6) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The scorch drake makes one Bite and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) piercing and 3 (1d6) fire damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing and 3 (1d6) fire damage.

action.name: Fire Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The scorch drake spits liquid fire in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 15 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 24 (7d6) fire damage and ignites. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and doesn’t ignite. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, an ignited creature takes 3 (1d6) fire damage at the start of each of its turns. This fire ignites flammable objects in the line that aren’t being worn or carried.

bonus.name: Fan the Flames
entries: The scorch drake flaps its wings in the direction of one ignited creature it can see within 15 feet of it. The flames coating the target flare up and generate excess smoke. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be blinded until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: The drider is resistant to the charmed condition, and magic can’t put the drider to sleep.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The drider is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The drider can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the drider has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 10 when perceiving by sight.

trait.name: Web Sense
entries: While in contact with a web, the drider knows the exact location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

trait.name: Web Walker
entries: The drider ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The drider makes one Longsword attack and two Skewer attacks, or it makes three Web Shot attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) slashing damage, or 8 (1d10 + 3) slashing damage if used with two hands.

action.name: Skewer
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage plus 13 (3d8) poison damage.

action.name: Web Shot
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 30/60 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d10 + 3) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be restrained by webbing. A creature, including the restrained target, can take its action to break the webbing and free the restrained target by succeeding on a DC 15 STR check.

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The dryad is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The dryad has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: One with the Forest
entries: While in a forest, the dryad can’t be surprised, and it leaves behind no tracks or other traces of its passage.

trait.name: Speak with Beasts and Plants
entries: The dryad can communicate with Beasts and Plants as if they shared a language.

trait.name: Tree Stride
entries: Once on its turn, the dryad can use 10 feet of its movement to step magically into one living tree within its reach and emerge from a second living tree within 60 feet of the first tree, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second tree. Both trees must be Large or larger.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dryad makes two Vine Whip or Blast of Pollen attacks. If both Vine Whip attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 14 STR save or be restrained by vines until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Vine Whip
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) slashing damage.

action.name: Blast of Pollen
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) poison damage.

bonus.name: Charming Scent
entries: The dryad emits an alluring, floral scent toward one Beast or Humanoid it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 14 WIS save or be charmed for 1 minute. While charmed, the target views the dryad as an ally and views the dryad’s requests favorably, though it isn’t under the dryad’s control. The charmed creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The dryad can have up to two creatures charmed in this way at a time. Forest’s Aid (21 HP or Fewer, 1/Day). The dryad magically calls a horde of squirrels, rabbits, mice, and other small animals native to the forest. The horde uses the statistics of 1 swarm of rats. The called creatures arrive on initiative count 20 of the next round, acting as allies of the dryad and obeying its spoken commands. They remain for 1 hour, until the dryad dies, or until the dryad dismisses them as a bonus action.

trait.name: Duergar Resilience
entries: The duergar is resistant to the charmed and paralyzed conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells and illusions.

trait.name: Reduce
entries: If the duergar starts its turn enlarged, it can choose to end the effect and return to its normal size (no action required).

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the duergar has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 5 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: War Pick
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage, or 11 (2d8 + 2) piercing damage while enlarged.

action.name: Javelin
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage, or 9 (2d6 + 2) piercing damage while enlarged.

action.name: Invisibility
entries: The duergar magically turns invisible until it attacks or uses Enlarge, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the duergar wears or carries is invisible with it.

bonus.name: Enlarge
entries: The duergar magically increases in size, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying. While enlarged, the duergar is Large, doubles its damage dice on weapon attacks (included in the attacks), and makes STR checks and saves with advantage. While enlarged, it also can no longer use the Invisibility action. If the duergar lacks the room to become Large, this bonus action fails.

reaction.name: Shield Wall
entries: The duergar adds 2 to its AC against one weapon attack that would hit it. To do so, the duergar must be wielding a shield and within 5 feet of another duergar that is also wielding a shield.

trait.name: Air Form
entries: The elemental can enter a hostile creature’s space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Cloud Camouflage
entries: The elemental’s Stealth is 20 while in clouds, fog, or windy weather.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The elemental doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Elemental Resilience
entries: The elemental is resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, and unconscious conditions.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The elemental makes two Wind Lash or Lightning Bolt attacks.

action.name: Wind Lash
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Lightning Bolt
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) lightning damage.

action.name: Whirlwind (Recharge 4–6)
entries: Each creature in the elemental’s space must make a DC 13 STR save. On a failure, a target takes 18 (3d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage and is flung up 20 feet away from the elemental in a random direction and knocked prone. If a thrown target strikes an object, such as a wall or floor, the target takes 3 (1d6) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a DC 13 DEX save or take the same damage and be knocked prone. If the save is successful, the target takes half the bludgeoning damage and isn’t flung away or knocked prone.

reaction.name: Thunderous Retreat
entries: When a creature the air elemental can see deals damage to it, the elemental can expel a crack of thunder and move up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Each creature within 5 feet of the space the elemental left must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or take 7 (2d6) thunder damage.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The elemental can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the elemental doesn’t disturb the material through which it moves.

trait.name: Earthen Camouflage
entries: The elemental’s Stealth is 14 while in muddy, rocky, sandy, or stony terrain.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The elemental doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Elemental Resilience
entries: The elemental is resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The elemental deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The elemental makes three Slam attacks, or it makes two Lob Stone attacks. If two Slam or Lob Stone attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Lob Stone
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (3d10 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Earth Tremor (Recharge 4–6)
entries: The earth elemental causes the ground on a point it can see within 30 feet of it to rumble and shake violently. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 16 DEX save or be knocked prone.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The fire elemental doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Elemental Resilience
entries: The fire elemental is resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Fire Camouflage
entries: The fire elemental’s Stealth is 18 while in a Medium or larger fire.

trait.name: Fire Form
entries: The elemental can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. A creature that touches the elemental or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 5 (1d10) fire damage. In addition, the elemental can enter a hostile creature’s space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature’s space on a turn, that creature must succeed on a DC 14 DEX save or take 5 (1d10) fire damage and catch fire; until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the burning creature takes 5 (1d10) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The elemental sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet.

trait.name: Water Susceptibility
entries: For every 5 feet the elemental moves in water, or for every gallon of water splashed on it, it takes 1 cold damage.

trait.name: Elemental Nature
entries: The elemental doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Elemental Resilience
entries: The elemental is resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Freeze
entries: If the elemental takes cold damage, it partially freezes, and its speed is reduced by 20 feet until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Purify Water
entries: If the water elemental sits in a body of slow-flowing or standing water, such as a spring, small creek, or lake, for at least 1 hour, the water becomes purified and rendered free of poison and disease. In slow-flowing water, this purification fades 1 hour after the elemental leaves the water. In standing water, this purification lasts until a contaminant enters the water while the elemental isn’t in it.

trait.name: Water Form
entries: The elemental can enter a hostile creature’s space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: The drow is resistant to the charmed condition, and magic can’t put the drow to sleep.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the drow has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 7 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Hand Crossbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Poison Weapon
entries: The drow coats one weapon it is wearing or carrying in one of the following poisons. Each time the drow hits a creature with that weapon, the target must succeed on a DC 11 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute and suffer the chosen poison’s effect while poisoned in this way. The poisoned creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The poison remains on the weapon until the start of the drow’s next turn.
• Beetle’s Bite. The target’s speed is halved while poisoned.
• Dark Caress. The target is blinded while poisoned.
• Nightmare’s Kiss. The target is frightened of the drow
while poisoned.
• Slumber’s Call. The target falls unconscious while
poisoned or until it takes damage or another creature uses an action to wake it.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The ettercap is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ettercap can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Web Sense
entries: While in contact with a web, the ettercap knows the exact location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

trait.name: Web Walker
entries: The ettercap ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ettercap makes one Bite attack and one Claws attack, or it makes two Spit Poison attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) poison damage. The target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Spit Poison
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 3) poison damage.

bonus.name: Web (Recharge 4–6)
entries: The ettercap launches a gob of webbing at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 DEX save or be restrained. A creature, including the restrained target, can take its action to free the target from the webbing by succeeding on a DC 13 STR check. The webbing can also be attacked and destroyed (AC 10; HP 5; vulnerability to fire damage; immunity to bludgeoning, poison, and psychic damage).

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The ettin is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

trait.name: Multiple Heads
entries: The ettin has two heads and is resistant to the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Obstinate
entries: The ettin has advantage on WIS and CHA saves.

trait.name: Wakeful
entries: When one of the ettin’s heads is asleep, its other head is awake.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ettin makes one Knobbed Club attack and one Spiked Club attack.

action.name: Knobbed Club
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn, as the blow knocks the wind out of the target. This weapon is wielded by the right head.

action.name: Spiked Club
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute, as bits of rotten meat clinging to the club’s spikes coat the target’s wound. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. This weapon is wielded by the left head.

bonus.name: Text
entries: That One! One of the ettin’s heads picks a creature the ettin can see within 30 feet of it, gaining advantage on its next attack against that creature. The other head doesn’t like being told what to do and has disadvantage on its next attack against that creature. Each weapon attack notes which head controls that weapon.

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The feral hunter is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Heightened Senses
entries: The feral hunter’s Perception is 19 when perceiving by sight, hearing, or smell.

trait.name: Precise
entries: A weapon that deals piercing damage deals one extra die of its damage when the feral hunter hits with it (included in the attack).

trait.name: Woodland Walk
entries: Difficult terrain composed of nonmagical plants doesn’t cost the feral hunter extra movement. In addition, the feral hunter can pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

reaction.name: Reposition
entries: When the feral hunter takes damage, it can move up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

monster.name: Fey Guardian
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The fey guardian is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Guardian
entries: The fey guardian is bound to one Humanoid, which it serves as its ward. If the guardian’s ward dies, the guardian also dies. The guardian instinctively knows the location, physical state, and emotional state of its ward, provided they are on the same plane of existence. If the ward is on a different plane, the guardian knows which plane but not the exact location. In addition, the two can communicate telepathically over any distance, even when not on the same plane.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The fey guardian has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The flinderbeast is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Heightened Hearing and Smell
entries: The flinderbeast’s Perception 18 when perceiving by hearing or smell.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) acid damage.

action.name: Acid Spit
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) acid damage.

action.name: Breath of Flinders (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The flinderbeast exhales splinters in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 14 DEX save, taking 18 (4d8) piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Short Step
entries: The flinderbeast teleports to an unoccupied space it can see within 15 feet of it.

reaction.name: Catch Snack
entries: When the flinderbeast is the target of a ranged weapon attack that uses ammunition made mostly of wood, such as an arrow, the flinderbeast can attempt to catch and eat the ammunition. The flinderbeast must make a DC 14 DEX save. On a success, it gains 3 (1d6) temporary HP and prevents the attack’s damage.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the shrieker remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary fungus.

trait.name: Plant Resilience
entries: The shrieker is resistant to exhaustion and to the paralyzed, petrified, and unconscious conditions.

action.name: Thunderous Burst
entries: The shrieker emits a concentrated burst of sound at one creature it can sense within 30 feet of it. The target must make a DC 10 DEX save, taking 2 (1d4) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Shriek
entries: The shrieker emits a high-pitched shriek audible within 300 feet of it. Each creature within 30 feet of it that isn’t a Plant and that can hear the shriek must succeed on a DC 10 WIS save or be frightened until the shriek ends. The shrieker must use a bonus action on its subsequent turns to continue shrieking. It can stop shrieking at any time. The shriek ends if the shrieker is incapacitated. A frightened creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. A creature that successfully saves is immune to the frightened effect of this shrieker’s Shriek for the next 24 hours.

reaction.name: Startled Shriek
entries: When bright light or a creature other than a Plant moves to within 30 feet of the shrieker, the shrieker can use Shriek, if it isn’t already shrieking.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the violet fungus remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary fungus.

trait.name: Plant Resilience
entries: The violet fungus is resistant to exhaustion and to the paralyzed, petrified, and unconscious conditions.

action.name: Rotting Touch
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 5 (2d4) necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Text
entries: Necrotic Spores (8 HP

bonus.name: or Fewer)
entries: The violet fungus sends out a burst of necrotic-laced spores, rapidly rotting nearby flesh. Each creature within 10 feet of the violet fungus that isn’t a Construct must make a DC 10 CON save, taking 5 (2d4) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Diving Pounce
entries: If the gargoyle is flying and moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claws attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the gargoyle can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the gargoyle remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an inanimate statue.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gargoyle makes one Bite attack and one Claws attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage.

reaction.name: Fearsome Mien
entries: When a creature the gargoyle can see hits it with an attack, the gargoyle can shift its face to appear more monstrous. The attacker must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or be frightened of the gargoyle until the end of its next turn.
