legendaryGroups.name: Aboleth
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing all initiative ties), the aboleth can use a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the aboleth can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Mirrors of Beyond. The water around up to two points the aboleth can see within 120 feet of it becomes unnaturally ebullient, beguiling nearby creatures with visions of a hypnotic, alien world. Each creature that starts its turn within 20 feet of a point and can see the water must make a DC 16 CHA save. Aberrations, Undead, and water-based Elementals automatically succeed on the save. On a failed save, the creature takes 11 (2d10) psychic damage and must use a reaction to move up to its speed toward the nearest point, trying to get within 5 feet of it. The creature takes the safest available route to the point, and the effect ends when the creature is within 5 feet of the point or has no more movement.

Tidal Grip. Watery tendrils erupt from up to three points of water the aboleth can see within 120 feet of it, grabbing at nearby creatures. Each creature within 10 feet of a point must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or be restrained by the watery tendril. While restrained, the creature can’t breathe, unless it can breathe water. A creature, including the target, can take its action to free the target from the tendril by succeeding on a DC 16 STR check. If the aboleth uses this lair action again to make new watery tendrils, all existing watery tendrils disappear, freeing their targets.

Terrorize. A wave of psychic energy washes out from a point the aboleth can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 16 INT save or be frightened for 1 minute. While frightened, a creature has disadvantage on CON saves made to maintain concentration on a spell, and it takes 7 (2d6) psychic damage at the start of each of its turns. The frightened creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The area near an aboleth’s lair is warped by its alien presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Clouded Vision. A milky film forms over the eyes of each creature that finishes a long rest within 3 miles of the aboleth’s lair. An afflicted creature has disadvantage on WIS (Perception) checks until it finishes a long rest more than 3 miles away from the lair or until the aboleth dies.

Elder Oil. Pungent, slippery, flavorless oil seeps from natural and carved stone within 1 mile of the lair. Stone and underground surfaces in the area are difficult terrain. Nonmagical fire within 10 feet of the oil sputters with a green flame, and the radius of the light it sheds is halved.

Living Waters. Nonmagical bodies of water within 10 miles of the aboleth’s lair ripple and writhe as if they are alive. Checks made to steer, control, or navigate a water vehicle are made with disadvantage while within 10 miles of the lair.

regionalEffects: If the aboleth dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Solar
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the solar can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the solar can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Dancing Weapons. Weapons of light in the shape of swords, axes, maces, and similar objects appear around up to three creatures the solar can see within 120 feet of it. The solar makes one ranged attack roll (+13 to hit) against each target. On a hit, the target takes 14 (4d6) radiant damage.

Divine Echoes. The sounds of trumpets, horns, drums, and other musical instruments of battle resound within the lair. Each hostile creature the solar can see within 60 feet of it must succeed on a DC 22 CON save or take 7 (2d6) thunder damage. Each friendly creature the solar can see within 60 feet of it regains 7 (2d6) HP. Fiends and Undead that fail this save are vulnerable to radiant damage until initiative count 20 on the next round.

Erase Shadows. Each object within 60 feet of the solar that isn’t being worn or carried sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet until initiative count 20 on the next round. This light is sunlight. Each hostile creature in this light must succeed on a DC 22 DEX save or take 7 (2d6) radiant damage, be blinded until the end of its next turn, and have disadvantage on DEX (Stealth) checks while in the light. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a solar’s lair is suffused with its blazing righteousness, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Calming Presence. Wild Beasts within 5 miles of the lair aren’t hostile unless attacked, and domestic Beasts, such as horses and oxen, within 5 miles of the lair are easier to handle, granting creatures advantage on WIS (Animal Handling) checks when interacting with the Beasts.

Domain of Justice. Within 3 miles of the lair, creatures have disadvantage on CHA (Deception) checks. In addition, when a creature with an INT of 6 or higher attacks an unarmed creature within 3 miles of the lair, the attacker must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or the attack fails.

Tactician’s Gaze. While in its lair, the solar can choose to see and hear through creatures within 1 mile of its lair that have divine power or a divine connection of some kind, such as Celestials, clerics, paladins, aasimars, and similar creatures, and the solar can telepathically communicate with such a creature.

regionalEffects: If the solar dies, these effects end immediately. If the solar leaves the area and establishes a new lair elsewhere, these effects fade in 1d6 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Black Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Acid Blister. Acid wells up from the ground on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it, covering the ground within 15 feet of that point. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or take 7 (2d6) acid damage. The blister lasts for 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies.

Blinding Filth. Swamp gas, muck, and slime spew up from a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be blinded until it or a creature within reach of it takes an action to wipe the filth from its eyes.

Sucking Mud. The ground beneath up to three creatures the dragon can see within 120 feet of it turns to soft mud. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be restrained by the mud. A creature, including the restrained target, can take its action to free the restrained creature by succeeding on a DC 15 STR check. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a black dragon’s lair is warped by its magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Relentless Insects. Within 5 miles of the lair, biting flies and stinging insects relentlessly assault Humanoids and Giants. Such creatures have disadvantage on DEX (Stealth) and WIS (Survival) checks while in the area. In addition, Humanoids and Giants that finish a short or long rest in the area must succeed on a DC 10 CON save or gain no benefit from the rest. A creature that spends the rest protected from the insects automatically succeeds on the save.

Rising Bile. A creature that finishes a long rest within 10 miles of the lair is plagued by bitter, acrid indigestion after every meal until it finishes a long rest outside the area. A creature that spends 7 days in the area suffers this effect until cured with the restoration spell or similar magic.

Rotten Fog. Within 1 mile of the lair, the air is filled with a thick, oily fog that reeks of sulfur and rotten flesh. Creatures automatically fail WIS (Perception) checks that rely on scent, and standing water in the area is blighted and not safe to drink for any creature other than the dragon.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Blue Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Create Conduit. One metal object or one creature wearing metal armor (the dragon’s choice) within 120 feet of the dragon becomes a conduit of lightning. If the target is an unwilling creature, it can make a DC 15 CON save, avoiding the effect on a success. Whenever a creature ends its turn within 10 feet of the conduit, that creature and the conduit each take 5 (2d4) lightning damage. The target remains a conduit for 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies.

Dust Gale. A gust of strong, sandy wind blows through the lair in a 50-foot line that is 10 feet wide and originating from a point the dragon can see within 60 feet of it. Each creature in the line must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be pushed up to 25 feet in a direction following the line. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more is also blinded until the end of its next turn.

Upheaval. Columns of sand burst upward beneath up to two creatures the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be hurled up to 30 feet into the air. If a target strikes a solid surface on the way up, it takes 3 (1d6) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was hurled upward before striking the surface. A target that can’t fly falls back to the ground, lands prone, taking falling damage, and is buried in sand. If the buried target doesn’t have a burrowing speed, it is restrained and unable to breathe or stand up. A creature, including the buried target, can take an action to free the buried target by succeeding on a DC 15 STR check. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a blue dragon’s lair is warped by its magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Collapsing Ground. Sinkholes appear randomly in the sand within 1 mile of the lair. Each hour a creature travels in the area, it must succeed on a DC 11 DEX save or fall into a sinkhole. Sinkholes range from 10 feet deep to up to 30 feet deep and disappear within 24 hours of appearing.
Magnetized Metal. Ferrous metal within 3 miles of the lair occasionally becomes magnetized for a short time, sticking to other ferrous metal objects near it.
Raging Storms. Thunderstorms and sandstorms occur regularly within 5 miles of the lair. While in such a storm, creatures have disadvantage on WIS (Perception) checks made to see or hear.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Brass Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Heat Blast. A ripple of heat surrounds up to three creatures the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be overcome by heat until the end of its next turn. A target overcome by heat must spend its action attempting to cool itself.

Nightmare Slumber. Nightmares fill the minds of up to two unconscious creatures the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or be wracked with nightmares while it remains unconscious. An unconscious creature wracked with nightmares takes 5 (2d4) psychic damage at the start of each of its turns. The damage from these nightmares doesn’t wake an unconscious creature. The dragon learns one small secret about an unconscious creature each time it takes psychic damage from this effect.

Sand Squall. A 20-foot-radius sphere of sand-filled wind appears centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. Ranged weapon attacks that pass through the sphere fail, and unprotected nonmagical flames in the sphere are extinguished. In addition, a creature other than the dragon that flies into the sphere or starts its turn flying in the sphere must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be knocked prone. The squall lasts for 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a brass dragon’s lair is warped by its magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Boiling Heat. Within 1 mile of the dragon’s desert lair, the heat is unnaturally oppressive. Each hour a creature spends in the area it must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or suffer one level of exhaustion.

Guiding Winds. Within 10 miles of the dragon’s desert lair, gentle breezes and wisps of cloud unerringly blow toward water sources and shelter. These winds gust stronger the closer a creature is to the dragon’s lair. Creatures following such wind have advantage on saves to avoid exhaustion brought on by thirst, starvation, or the desert’s heat.

Oasis Sense. Small oases appear within 20 miles of the dragon’s humanoid lair. Each oasis has a water spring, enough food (including fruit and insects) to support up to 10 Medium or smaller creatures every 24 hours, and shelter in the form of small trees and bushes. The dragon knows the distance, direction, and size of any creature that enters an oasis. Creatures that drink water or eat food from any such oasis have disadvantage on saves against the dragon’s Sleep Breath for 24 hours.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Bronze Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Geyser. A spray of water in an 80-foot line that is 10 feet wide flies out of a point on a surface the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature in that line must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or take 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage, be pushed up to 40 feet in a direction following the line, and be knocked prone.

Haze. A 40-foot-radius sphere of hazy mist appears centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. Any part of the sphere underwater turns the water murky and is difficult terrain. A creature that starts its turn in the sphere must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be poisoned until the start of its next turn. The sphere lasts for 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies.

Thunderburst. A burst of thunder and lightning strikes up to two creatures the dragon can see within 60 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or take 7 (2d6) lightning damage and 7 (2d6) thunder damage and be deafened until the end of its next turn. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more is also stunned until the end of its next turn. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a bronze dragon’s lair is warped by its magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Encourage Brilliance. Each decade, the dragon chooses one overarching aspect of humanoid growth and progress to encourage, such as social, magical, martial, economic, or similar. Within 20 miles of the dragon’s lair, each humanoid settlement experiences slow, subtle, and steady growth and progress in the chosen area, which might appear in the form of a blacksmith developing a more efficient technique for creating armor, a boom in births among the working class, a breakthrough in research in a magical university, or similar.

Seafarer’s Visage. Within 10 miles of the dragon’s lair, sailors can see ghostly figures in the water. The figures gesture for the sailors to follow, encouraging merchants and other friendly vessels safely through dangerous waters and toward settlements and coaxing brigands and other unfriendly vessels toward rocky shoals or away from settlements.

Twinkling Coral. Coral reefs within 3 miles of the dragon’s lair glow dimly at night, signaling their presence to sailors. This glow brightens during dark, coastal storms. A creature piloting a ship has advantage on checks to navigate the ship safely through or around the glowing coral reefs.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Copper Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Acrid Zephyr. A 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-tall cylinder of caustic mist appears centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or take 9 (2d8) acid damage. The mist lasts for 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies.

Arsenal Barrage. Dozens of axes, swords, daggers, hammers, and other weapons clash together on a point the dragon can see within 60 feet of it, parrying, dodging, and otherwise moving as if held by invisible combatants. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must make a DC 15 DEX save, taking 4 (1d8) bludgeoning damage, 2 (1d4) piercing damage, and 3 (1d6) slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Inspire Valor. A desire for combat wells up within up to three creatures the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each target has advantage on the first attack roll it makes on its turn, and if that attack hits, the attack deals an extra 4 (1d8) acid damage. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a copper dragon’s lair is warped by its magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Defensible. Natural land features, such as hills, stone spires, and crevasses, within 3 miles of the dragon’s lair are more easily defensible, with single-entry caverns, juts of rocky outcroppings for cover, and similar characteristics.

Duels. Creatures with an Intelligence of 5 or higher within 5 miles of the lair are prone to challenge each other to friendly duels, challenges of skill, challenges of wit, and similar contests to resolve most minor issues.

Toxic Vapors. Within 1 mile of the dragon’s lair, toxic vapors bubble up from cracks in the ground, repelling insects, rodents, and other small vermin. In addition, a creature that finishes a long rest in a cavern or after sleeping on the ground in the area has minor headaches throughout the day until it finishes a long rest outside the area.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, the defensible effect fades after 1 year, but the other effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Gold Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Blinding Light. A brilliant flash of scorching light surrounds up to three creatures the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or take 13 (3d8) fire damage and be blinded until the end of its next turn.

Boundless Wisdom. The wisdom of generations briefly fills the mind of one creature the dragon can see within 60 feet of it. If the target is friendly, it has advantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saves until initiative count 20 of the next round, as its body moves and reacts from generations of honed training. If the target is hostile, it must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn, as its mind is overwhelmed by the flood of information.

Rumble. The ground rumbles on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature on the ground within 20 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or take 9 (2d8) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a gold dragon’s lair is warped by its magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Fortuitous Knowledge. Within 10 miles of the dragon’s lair, creatures seeking knowledge with genuine, passionate hearts find the knowledge a bit easier to obtain: the book with the information they seek lies open on a library table, the person who knows the information is having lunch nearby, or other similar fortuitous coincidences.

Enlightening Influence. Within 1 mile of the dragon’s lair, a creature that attempts to speak a lie must succeed on a DC 13 CHA save or speak the truth instead. In addition, a creature that finishes a long rest in the area has the subtle urge to better itself physically, mentally, or magically through training and self-reflection.

Savior Beacon. Within 5 miles of the dragon’s lair, a mote of gentle, warm light guides kindly travelers safely through dangerous areas, and a similar light appears and signals help for wounded or stranded creatures.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Green Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Alchemical Spray. A spray of toxic slime erupts from the ground or drips from the ceiling at a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or take 7 (2d6) poison damage and be poisoned for 1 minute. While poisoned, a creature that is resistant to poison damage isn’t resistant to poison damage. A poisoned creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Disorienting Gas. A sickly sweet, colorless gas wafts across the senses of up to three creatures the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be disoriented until the end of its next turn. When a disoriented creature moves, it moves in a random direction.

Thorny Plants. Thorny vines grow in a 20-foot radius on the ground centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The area is difficult terrain, and when a creature moves into or within the area, it takes 5 (2d4) piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels. The vines last for 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a green dragon’s lair is warped by its magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Overgrowth. Plants within 3 miles of the dragon’s lair grow unnaturally tangled and thick and produce a variety of plant-based alchemical reagents, both local and exotic. The entire area is difficult terrain for all except the dragon. Poison Breath (Recharge 5–6). The dragon exhales poisonous gas in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 11 CON save, taking 21 (6d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Young Green Dragon

Spark of Inspiration. When a creature within 10 miles of the dragon’s lair performs an alchemical experiment with unknown results for the first time, the experiment yields one and a half times as much as it normally would, or the experiment’s results are otherwise 50 percent more potent than expected. Speed 40 ft., fly 80 ft., swim 40 ft.

Tainted Water. The dragon knows when a creature with an Intelligence of 5 or higher drinks from a pond, pool, spring, or other water source within 5 miles of its lair, and the dragon can choose for that water to be poisonous to the creature. If it does so, the creature must succeed on a DC 11 CON save or be poisoned for 24 hours. +4

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Red Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Collapse. The ceiling on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it collapses, covering a 20-foot square area on the ground directly below that point. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be knocked prone and buried under the rubble. A buried creature is restrained and unable to breathe or stand up. A creature, including the buried creature, can take its action to free the buried creature by succeeding on a DC 15 STR check while within reach of the buried creature.

Swirling Ash. Searing ash blasts a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 30 feet of that point must make a DC 15 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 10 (3d6) fire damage and is blinded until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t blinded. The ash ignites flammable objects within 30 feet of that point that aren’t being worn or carried.

Conflagrate. One creature the dragon can see within 120 feet of it becomes tethered to the Elemental Plane of Fire. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or be burned for 1 minute. While burned, the target takes 7 (2d6) fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The effect ends early if the target takes cold damage, is submerged in water, or the burn is removed with a greater restoration spell or similar magic.

regionalEffects: The region containing a red dragon’s lair is suffused with fiery chaos, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Acid Rain. Within 10 miles of the red dragon’s lair, precipitation is oily and stinks of sulfur. Snow in the area may be gray or black as it falls, appearing like ash, though its temperature is unchanged.

Incited Rage. Creatures within 3 miles of the red dragon’s lair are quick to anger and slow to forgive. Such creatures have disadvantage on CHA checks, except CHA (Intimidation), and Beasts in the area that aren’t domesticated are more aggressive than normal.

Unnatural Heat. Within 1 mile of the red dragon’s lair, temperatures are unnaturally high. Vegetation in the area withers and eventually crumbles to dust. Creatures in the area require twice as much water as normal each day to survive, and each creature in the area must succeed on a DC 10 CON save every hour it remains in the area or suffer one level of exhaustion. Creatures that are resistant or immune to fire damage are unaffected by this regional effect.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Silver Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Enthralling Reflection. Icy surfaces within the lair shine mirror bright. Each creature within 20 feet of a point on a frozen surface the dragon can see within 120 feet of it must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or be charmed until initiative count 20 of the following round. While charmed, a creature is incapacitated, and if it is more than 5 feet from the surface, it must move on its turn toward the surface, trying to get within 5 feet. It doesn’t avoid opportunity attacks or hazardous terrain. If a charmed creature takes damage, the charmed effect ends for that creature.

Flash Freeze. Up to two targets the dragon can see within 120 feet of it must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be restrained for 1 minute as its body begins to freeze. At the end of each of its turns, it can repeat the save. On a success, the effect ends for the creature. If the creature fails this save three times, it is petrified in ice until the condition is removed.

Blizzard. The dragon creates a 20-foot-radius sphere of driving snow centered on a point it can see within 120 feet. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. Each creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed a DC 15 CON save or take 7 (2d6) cold damage and its speed is halved until the end of its turn. The sphere lasts until the dragon uses this lair action again or until the dragon dies. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a silver dragon’s lair is warped by its magical presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Welcoming Chill. Humanoids within 10 miles of the dragon’s lair remain comfortable in temperatures as low as −50 degrees Fahrenheit without any additional protection and can tolerate temperatures as low as −100 degrees Fahrenheit while wearing heavy clothes. The dragon can end this effect at will for any Humanoid it knows is within 10 miles of its lair.

Ice Surveillance. Within 3 miles of the dragon’s lair, the dragon can choose to see and hear through frozen ponds and puddles, icicles on buildings, columns of ice, ice sculptures, and similar areas of frozen water, and the dragon can speak through such ice while doing so.

Disorienting Snow. Snow flurries dance through the area within 1 mile of the dragon’s lair. A Humanoid hostile to the dragon and in this area must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save for every 10 minutes it travels through the area, becoming lost on a failed save. A lost Humanoid returns to the spot where it entered the area within 1 mile of the dragon’s lair, regardless of how far or for how long it traveled.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Void Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Orb of the Void. The Void briefly overlaps the dragon’s lair in a 20-foot-radius sphere of blackness punctuated by deep blue streaks and pinpoints of light. The sphere is centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of the dragon. The sphere spreads around corners, is heavily obscured, and contains no air (creatures must hold their breath). Each creature in the sphere when it appears must make a DC 15 CON save, taking 7 (2d6) cold damage and 7 (2d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Any creature that ends its turn in the sphere takes 7 (2d6) cold damage and 7 (2d6) necrotic damage. The sphere lasts 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies.

Spatial Anomaly. The lair’s connection to the Void briefly rips the fabric of space, forcing two creatures the dragon can see within 120 feet of it to suddenly exist at the same point in space and time. The laws of reality repel the creatures back to their original positions as reality resets. The targets aren’t actually moved, but reality’s reset forces each target to make a DC 15 STR save. On a failure, the creature takes 16 (3d10) force damage and is knocked prone. On a success, the creature takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone.

Zero Gravity. Gravity stops working in a 50-foot radius, 100-foot-high cylinder centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature in that cylinder drifts 10 feet away from the ground and must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be restrained. A flying creature automatically succeeds on the save, but its speed is halved unless it can hover or flies magically. This effect lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a void dragon’s lair is warped by the dragon’s magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Dark of the Void. Nonmagical illumination, including sunlight, can’t create bright light within 6 miles of the lair.

Torn Veil. Within 3 miles of the lair, disembodied voices whisper in the night. Celestials, Fey, and Fiends with a challenge rating of 2 or lower can choose to slip into the Material Plane from their native planes or vice versa.

Uncovered Knowledge. Secrets have a way of coming to light within 6 miles of the lair. Clues are inadvertently discovered, slips of the tongue hint at a hidden truth, and creatures become morbidly curious for forbidden knowledge.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: White Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Blizzard. Whipping winds and snow appear in the shape of a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The blizzard’s area is heavily obscured. A creature that enters the blizzard for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or take 7 (2d6) cold damage. A creature flying in or through the blizzard must land on its turn or be knocked prone.

Frozen Treat. One creature the dragon can see within 60 feet of it begins to freeze solid. The target must make a DC 15 CON save. On a success, the target is restrained until the end of its next turn. On a failure, the target is petrified in ice for 1 minute. A petrified creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. A petrified creature that takes fire damage has advantage on the save to end the effect.

Ice Slick. A 40-foot radius patch of ground centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it freezes. The ice slick is difficult terrain, and each time a creature moves more than 5 feet in or through the slick must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or fall prone. The ice slick lasts for 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies.

Thundershift. The weather in the dragon’s lair suddenly shifts, causing a thunderous explosion on a point the dragon can see within 90 feet of it. Each creature within 20 feet of that point must make a DC 15 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 7 (2d6) cold damage and 7 (2d6) thunder damage and is knocked prone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a white dragon’s lair is warped by its magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Frigid Cold. Blizzards continually rage within 3 miles of the dragon’s lair, making the area nearly inhospitable for creatures not resistant to cold damage.

Hunting Grounds. The area within 5 miles of the dragon’s lair is its hunting ground. When a Large or larger creature enters the area, the dragon immediately knows the direction and general distance to that creature for the next hour.
Icy Land. Ice and hard-packed snow coat the area within 1 mile of the lair, making it difficult terrain.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Yellow Dragon
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), a yellow dragon can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Grasslands Cacophony. The sounds of thunder, howling wind, insects, and a chorus of rustling grasses suddenly roar from a point the dragon can see within 60 feet of it. Each creature within 15 feet of that point that can hear the cacophony must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be deafened and stunned until the end of its next turn.

Pitfall. A 20-foot-radius pit that is 20 feet deep opens in the ground centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or fall into the pit, taking falling damage as normal and landing prone. The pit remains for 1 minute, until the dragon uses this lair action again, or until the dragon dies. When the pit ends, its floor rises up to the level of the ground, harmlessly pushing any creatures in the pit back to the surface.

Radiant Flare. A flash of bright light bursts from a point in the air the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 30 feet of it that can see the light must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be blinded until the end of its next turn. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a yellow dragon’s lair is changed by its presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Calm Fires. Wildfires are less common within 10 miles of the dragon’s lair, and when a wildfire burns within 10 miles of the lair, it lasts half as long.
Fleeing Prey. Animals, especially prey animals, within 3 miles of the dragon’s lair are more skittish than normal and frequently run at the slightest movement or perceived danger.

Intense Sunlight. Within 1 mile of the dragon’s lair, the sun beats down more intensely than usual. During the day, each creature in the area, other than yellow dragons, must succeed on a DC 10 CON save every hour or suffer one level of exhaustion. A creature automatically succeeds on this save if it takes shelter from the sun or otherwise avoids direct sunlight.

regionalEffects: If the dragon dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Kraken
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the kraken can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the kraken can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Fulminate. The kraken’s lair becomes electrified. Up to four creatures the kraken can see within 120 feet of it must make a DC 22 CON save, taking 13 (3d8) lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more is also stunned until the end of its next turn.

Silt Cloud. A 60-foot-radius sphere of fine, swirling sand appears centered on a point the kraken can see within 120 feet of it. The sphere spreads around corners and is heavily obscured. The cloud remains for 10 minutes or until the kraken uses this lair action again.

Tidal Churn. Water shifts in the kraken’s lair. Up to four creatures the kraken can see within 60 feet of it that are touching water must succeed on a DC 22 STR save or be pushed or pulled up to 60 feet away from or toward the kraken (the kraken’s choice).

regionalEffects: The region containing a kraken’s lair is altered by its presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Dark Brine. Water within 5 miles of the kraken’s lair becomes supernaturally dark, silty, or otherwise lightly obscured.

Thunderous Waves. Within 10 miles of the kraken’s lair, waves and seaborn winds produce a deafening roar. Each hour a creature spends in the area, it must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be deafened. Creatures submerged in water or sand automatically succeed on the save. In addition, each creature that spends at least half of a short or long rest in the area and not submerged in water or sand must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or gain no benefit from the rest.

Tide Sense. The kraken is aware of the presence of any creature within 1 mile of the lair that remains in contact with water or sand for at least 1 hour. In addition, the kraken knows the distance and direction of each Celestial and Fiend in the area.

regionalEffects: If the kraken dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Lich
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the lich can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the lich can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Abjure Life. Each creature within 120 feet of the lich that isn’t undead can’t regain HP until initiative count 20 on the next round. For the duration, a creature that is reduced to 0 HP within 120 feet of the lich must immediately make a death save.

Shroud of the Dead. A blanket of necrotic energy falls on a point the lich can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 20 WIS save or be blinded and deafened and vulnerable to necrotic damage until initiative count 20 on the next round.

Unlife Tether. One creature the lich can see within 60 feet of it must succeed on a DC 20 CHA save or become magically tethered to the lich until initiative count 20 on the next round. While tethered, the lich is resistant to all damage dealt by the tethered creature, the tethered creature takes necrotic damage equal to half the damage taken by the lich, and if the tethered creature regains HP, the lich regains twice that amount. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a lich’s lair is warped by its influence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Scrambled Translocation. Within 1 mile of the lich’s lair, planar travel and teleportation are warped. When a creature other than the lich tries to teleport into, out of, or within the area, the creature must roll a d6. On a 1–3, the teleportation fails. On a roll of 4–6, a teleporting creature takes 11 (2d10) force damage and appears in an unoccupied space just outside of this area.

Spell Detection. The lich is intrinsically aware of and can identify any spell cast within 3 miles of its lair. In addition, if a creature casts a spell of 4th circle or higher, the lich knows the distance and direction of that creature for 1 hour.

Withering Fog. A magical fog permeates the area within 5 miles of the lich’s lair. The area is lightly obscured, and each creature that isn’t a Construct or Undead that finishes a long rest in the area must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or suffer one level of exhaustion.

regionalEffects: If the lich dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Virtuoso Lich
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the virtuoso lich takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the lich can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Artistic Assault. The virtuoso lich channels an artistic expression it can see or hear into a magical assault. The artistic expression must be of the type chosen with the lich’s Versatile Artist trait, but it otherwise can be any form of expression not originating from the lich, such as the song of nearby singers that echoes in the lair, the colorful paint decorating canvases, the twirling forms of dancers, or similar. The lich chooses a creature it can see within 30 feet of the artistic expression. The target must make a DC 17 DEX save, taking 18 (4d8) damage of the type chosen with the Versatile Artist trait on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Artistic Distraction. The natural artistry of the lich’s lair becomes suddenly enhanced, distracting and hindering nearby creatures. The lich chooses a point it can see within 60 feet of it. Each creature within 5 feet of that point must make a DC 17 CHA save. On a failure, a creature has disadvantage on saves against the lich’s spells and its Corrupted Art action until initiative count 20 on the next round.

Veil of Art. A shimmering veil of an artistic expression of the lich’s choice, such as dancing, music, swirling colors, or similar, appears on a point the lich can see within 60 feet of it. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 17 WIS save or be incapacitated until initiative count 20 on the next round, as it spends most of its turn mesmerized by the artistic expression. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a virtuoso lich’s lair is warped by its magical influence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Artistic Charm. Each nonhostile Beast within 1 mile of the lich’s lair that is exposed to an artist performing a form of art, such as dancing, playing music, or painting, must succeed on a DC 13 CHA save or become friendly toward the artist. The Beast doesn’t obey the artist’s commands, but it doesn’t attack the artist unless attacked by the artist or the artist’s allies.
Inspire Art. Creatures within 5 miles of the lich’s lair have advantage on CHA (Performance) checks when performing the art the lich has chosen that day as part of its Versatile Artist trait.

Vibrant Art. Artistic expressions within 10 miles of the lair are more vibrant: music has more body, paint colors are more vibrant, tailored clothing fits perfectly, and similar. Because of this vibrancy, Humanoids in the area and exposed to such art are more likely to be pleasant and in good moods and less likely to act aggressively.

regionalEffects: If the lich dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Mummy Lord
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the mummy lord can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the mummy lord can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Blast of Putrescence. A blast of putrescent wind assaults up to three creatures the mummy lord can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or take 7 (2d6) poison damage and be poisoned until the end of its next turn.

Blinding Sandstorm. A 10-foot-radius swirl of sand appears on a point the mummy lord can see within 60 feet of it. A creature that enters the swirl of sand for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 17 DEX save or be blinded until the start of its next turn. The swirl of sand lasts for 1 minute, until the mummy lord uses this lair action again, or until the mummy lord dies.

Swathe in Pain. One creature the mummy lord can see within 60 feet of it must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or be wracked with pain until the end of its next turn. While wracked with pain, the creature has disadvantage on saves, and if it casts a spell, it must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or the spell fails to cast, expending the action required to cast it but not the spell slot or daily use. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a mummy lord’s lair is altered by its deathly presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Allegiance. Creatures that inhabit the area within 10 miles of the mummy lord's lair or that spend at least 7 days in the area develop a strong sense of loyalty and love for the mummy lord's chosen people, nation, or deity.

Monument Surveillance. Within 3 miles of the mummy lord’s lair, the mummy lord can choose to see and hear through any statue or monument depicting itself, its blood descendants, or a symbol of the nation or deity associated with the mummy lord, such as a statue of the monarchy’s griffon insignia or a carved monument depicting the deity’s holy symbol.

Mummy’s Curse. Objects within 1 mile of the mummy lord’s lair and that belong to the mummy lord carry a powerful curse. A creature that takes such an object without the mummy lord’s permission becomes permanently cursed. While cursed, the creature ages twice as quickly, requires 12 hours to finish a long rest instead of 8, and has disadvantage on death saves. The creature remains cursed even if it no longer has the object, and each creature after the first that holds or carries the object for at least 24 hours also becomes cursed. The curse automatically ends on all creatures if the object is returned to within 1 mile of the mummy lord’s lair. The curse can be suppressed for 24 hours by a remove curse spell.

regionalEffects: If the mummy lord dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Sphinx
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the sphinx can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the sphinx can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Balance Time. Time around the sphinx becomes balanced. Each creature within 30 feet of the sphinx affected by a spell or other effect that lasts for more than 1 round has that effect’s duration reduced to 1 round. An unwilling creature must succeed on a DC 17 WIS save to avoid the reduction.

Enshroud. A 20-foot-radius sphere of magical darkness appears centered on a point the sphinx can see within 120 feet of it. While inside the sphere, the sphinx makes no noise unless it chooses to do so. The magical darkness lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round.

Sit and Listen. Up to three creatures the sphinx can see within 120 feet of it are pulled to the ground. Each target must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or be knocked prone and restrained until the end of its next turn. If a target fails the save while flying, it is immediately grounded, taking falling damage as normal, is knocked prone, and is restrained until the end of its next turn.

Transpose. Up to two creatures the sphinx can see within 120 feet of it switch places, each teleporting into the space of the other. The sphinx can’t target itself with this lair action. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a sphinx’s lair is altered by its presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Defensive Storms. Dust storms are more frequent within 5 miles of the sphinx’s lair. Creatures intentionally seeking the lair encounter such storms every 1d4 hours of travel in the area.

Guarded Secrets. A creature that finishes a short or long rest within 1 mile of the sphinx’s lair must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or forget any interaction it had with the sphinx that the sphinx doesn’t want it to remember.
Riddle Speak. For every 24 hours a creature spends within 2 miles of a sphinx’s lair, it must succeed on a DC 13 CHA save or speak only in riddles for 8 hours.

Truth Manifest. Within 3 miles of the sphinx’s lair, creatures have disadvantage on CHA (Deception) checks, and a creature that knowingly tells a lie suffers great pain for 1 minute. If a creature knowingly tells another lie while suffering this pain, it takes 2 (1d4) psychic damage. The sphinx is aware of the distance, direction, and identity of a creature that takes this damage.

regionalEffects: If the sphinx dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

legendaryGroups.name: Tarrasque
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the tarrasque can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the tarrasque can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Endless Hunger. The tarrasque’s nigh-endless hunger spreads to up to three creatures it can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 27 CON save or be overcome with hunger until initiative count 20 on the next round. A creature overcome with hunger can’t use reactions, and on its turn, it can use either an action or a bonus action, not both. Regardless of the creature’s abilities or magic items, a creature overcome with hunger can’t make more than one melee or ranged attack during its turn.

Growling Stomach. A rumble shakes the ground and an earthen maw opens on a point the tarrasque can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature on the ground within 30 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 27 DEX save or be knocked prone. Each creature that failed the save while within 10 feet of that point is also restrained as it becomes half-buried by the earth. A creature, including the restrained creature, can take an action to free the restrained creature by succeeding on a DC 27 STR check.

Overfull. The bloated sensation of an overfull belly overwhelms up to three creatures the tarrasque can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 27 CON save or feel overfull until initiative count 20 on the next round. A creature that feels overfull takes a −2 penalty to AC and DEX checks and DEX saves, and its speed is halved. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a tarrasque’s lair is warped by its hungering presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Food Aplenty. Flora and fauna, especially prey animals and crops, within 10 miles of the lair are more abundant, producing up to half again as much as normal animals and plants of their type.

Sated and Sleepy. Creatures near the lair become more complacent and sleepier after eating. A creature that eats a meal within 5 miles of the lair is vulnerable to the charmed and unconscious conditions for up to 1 hour after eating.
Seeping Hunger. The tarrasque’s hunger seeps into the countryside around its lair. Prepared food within 1 mile of the lair spoils faster, some of it magically consumed by the slumbering tarrasque.

regionalEffects: If the tarrasque dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days, except for Food Aplenty, which fades within 1 year.

legendaryGroups.name: Unicorn
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the unicorn can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the unicorn can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Grasping Vines. Vines reach out from the ground and trees in the lair toward up to two creatures the unicorn can see within 60 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 14 DEX save or take 5 (2d4) piercing damage and be restrained. While restrained, a creature takes 5 (2d4) piercing damage at the end of each of its turns. A creature, including the restrained creature, can take its action to free the restrained creature by succeeding on a DC 14 STR check.

Light Beam. A beam of sunlight or moonlight, whichever is appropriate for the time of day, shines down in a 10-foot-radius, 20-foot-high cylinder centered on a point the unicorn can see within 120 feet of it. A creature that enters the beam’s area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or take 7 (2d6) radiant damage. The unicorn can choose if this beam harms Beasts and Plants in its area. The beam lasts until the unicorn uses this lair action again or until the unicorn dies.

Shimmering Coat. All the light in the lair bends toward the unicorn, reflecting off its brilliant coat. Each hostile creature within 15 feet of the unicorn and that can see it must make a DC 14 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 14 (4d6) radiant damage and is blinded until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t blinded.

regionalEffects: The region containing a unicorn’s lair is altered by its protective presence, which creates one or more of the following effects.

items: Abundant Flora. Plant growth is more abundant within 1 mile of the unicorn’s lair, making the area difficult terrain for all except the unicorn.

Hinder Corruption. Aberrations and Undead within 3 miles of the lair are hindered while moving through the area. Roots rise to trip them, vines hang in their path, predators harry them, and similar small occurrences happen regularly, disrupting the creatures’ movement deeper into the forest.

Vibrant Fauna and Flora. The animals and plants within 10 miles of the lair are healthier, carry and suffer fewer diseases, and provide up to 50 percent more nourishment than standard animals and plants of their sizes and types to creatures that consume them.

legendaryGroups.name: Vampire
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the vampire can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the vampire can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items: Eclipse. Each source of nonmagical light within 30 feet of a point the vampire can see within 60 feet of it is extinguished. Each source of magical light within 30 feet of that point is diminished: bright light becomes dim light, and dim light becomes darkness. If the light is sunlight, it is diminished to bright light instead. This diminishing effect lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round.

Sanguine Tithe. Blood rains in a 30-foot radius, 60-foot-high cylinder centered on a point the vampire can see within 120 feet of it. Each hostile creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 17 DEX save or be coated in magical blood until initiative count 20 of the next round. Each time the vampire deals damage to a creature coated in magical blood, the vampire regains 5 (2d4) HP. The rain lasts for 1 minute, until the vampire uses this lair action, or until the vampire dies.

Shrieking Storm. A 30-foot radius cloud of shrieking bats briefly appears centered on a point the vampire can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature in the cloud must succeed on a DC 17 DEX save or be deafened until initiative count 20 on the next round. If a creature in the cloud is concentrating on a spell, the creature must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or lose concentration on the spell. REGIONAL EFFECTS

regionalEffects: The region containing a vampire’s lair is changed by its deathly presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items: Grave Visions. Within 5 miles of the lair, an illusion of the vampire occasionally hovers above graves, corpses, or piles of bones. The vampire knows the direction and distance to any creature that speaks to, touches, attacks, or otherwise interacts with such an illusion. Once touched or attacked, the illusion shatters into a swarm of bats or rats that remains for 1d4 rounds before scattering.

Healthy Stock. A Humanoid that spends at least 1 year within 5 miles of the lair is resistant to diseases and poisons while within 5 miles of the lair. This effect ends if the Humanoid spends more than 7 days outside this area.

Predator’s Temper. Within 10 miles of the lair, bats, rats, and wolves reflect the vampire’s current mood. They are more aggressive when the vampire is angry, friendlier when the vampire is happy, vocal with excessive howls and shrieks when the vampire is sullen, and similar.

regionalEffects: If the vampire dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.
